definition: The trajectory of fiscal impulse is the single most important monitoring variable for risk appetite regime determination. Persistent deficits at ~6-7% GDP sustain risk-on via Kalecki profit mechanics; fiscal consolidation >2pp erodes the income floor and accelerates Minsky dynamics.
evidence: Identified as single most important monitoring variable by panel consensus
Kalecki profit equation mechanics linking fiscal deficit to corporate earnings
Bifurcated scenario analysis showing divergent regime paths based on fiscal trajectory
open_questions: What is the political probability of fiscal consolidation >2pp?
How quickly does the Kalecki channel transmit fiscal changes to corporate profits?
Does deficit composition matter as much as the aggregate level?
